Adelphic Union
Lodge #14

A Prince Hall Affiliated Lodge in the heart of New York City. Rooted in tradition since 1863, we are guided by Faith, Hope, and Charity — building better men and a stronger community.

From the East

A Message from the Worshipful Master

W.M. Sean Baldon

I stand before you honored and deeply humbled by the trust you have placed in me to serve as your Worshipful Master. I accept this Charge with humility, determination, and a clear commitment to lead in a manner worthy of our Craft and our Lodge.

This Masonic year, we reaffirm our unwavering commitment to the virtues symbolized by the Acacia: renewal, integrity, devotion, and resilience. Standing upon the foundation laid by our Brothers since 1863, we move forward united, and directed by the Light that has always led this Lodge.

I accept this Station not in pursuit of recognition, but in service to this Lodge; first, last, and always. Every decision I make will be governed by what is best for 'The U' as a collective body; and I respectfully call upon each Brother for his continued counsel, cooperation, and support, for no Master labors alone.

We shall strive for excellence, not for praise, but because it is our solemn responsibility to ourselves, to one another, and to those who laid this foundation before us. No single Brother bears the full weight of our legacy; it is sustained through unity, discipline, and a shared purpose.

Brethren, let us walk together in harmony, deepen the ties that unite us, and commit ourselves to uplifting one another as we labor in love, relief, and truth. May every Brother, veteran, newly Raised, or returning home; be welcomed, empowered, and inspired to help carry this Lodge forward. I thank you, my Brothers, for your confidence and support.

Fraternally,
Bro. Sean M. Baldon
Worshipful Master

Established May 11, 1863
One of the oldest Prince Hall Masonic lodges in New York, serving the community for over 160 years through brotherhood, charitable works, and moral upliftment.
Our Heritage

A Legacy of Brotherhood & Service

Adelphic Union Lodge #14, under the jurisdiction of the Most Worshipful Prince Hall Grand Lodge of the State of New York, has stood as a beacon of fraternity, charity, and truth in Harlem and beyond since 1863.

Faith
Grounded in spiritual conviction and the enduring belief in a Supreme Being, guiding our moral compass in all endeavors.
Hope
An unwavering optimism for a just society, inspiring each brother to be a catalyst for positive change in our neighborhoods.
Charity
A commitment to benevolence and service, extending our hand to those in need through community programs and philanthropic works.
What We Do

Programs & Services

Strengthening our community through action, education, and brotherhood.

Masonic Education
Study groups, lectures, and workshops on the craft, its history, and the principles of Freemasonry within the Prince Hall tradition.
Community Outreach
Food drives, back-to-school programs, health fairs, and partnerships with local organizations serving Harlem and the greater NYC area.
Scholarships
Annual scholarship awards for deserving students, supporting the next generation of leaders committed to academic excellence.
Fellowship Events
Annual balls, family picnics, sports tournaments, and fraternal gatherings that strengthen bonds between brothers and families.
Charitable Works
Fundraising for local causes, support for veteran services, holiday giving campaigns, and hands-on volunteer initiatives.
Mentorship
Pairing seasoned brothers with newer members and young men in the community to foster personal growth and leadership skills.
Watch & Discover

Experience Lodge Life

See our brotherhood in action — from community service to fellowship events.

Lodge Highlight Reel
Video coming soon — subscribe to our newsletter for updates
Path to the Craft

How to Become a Mason

The journey begins with a sincere desire for self-improvement and service to others.

1

Express Your Interest

Reach out to us through our contact form or attend a public event. In Freemasonry, we do not recruit — a man must come of his own free will and accord.

2

Meet the Brothers

You will be invited to fellowship events and informal gatherings where you can learn about the lodge, ask questions, and get to know the members.

3

Submit a Petition

When you are ready, submit a formal petition for membership. A committee of brothers will conduct an investigation and interview.

4

Ballot & Initiation

The lodge votes on your petition. If elected, you will be initiated into the Entered Apprentice degree — beginning your journey through the craft.

5

Continue Your Journey

Progress through the Fellow Craft and Master Mason degrees, deepening your understanding of Masonic principles and commitment to brotherhood.

Ready to Take the First Step?

Freemasonry does not solicit members. You must ask. If you are a man of good character seeking to improve yourself and serve your community, we invite your inquiry.

Inquire About Membership
Belief in a Supreme Being
At least 18 years of age
Good moral character
Of your own free will

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

Answers to common questions about Freemasonry and our lodge.

What is Prince Hall Freemasonry?

Prince Hall Freemasonry is the oldest and largest fraternity for African American men in the world, founded by Prince Hall in 1784. It is a recognized branch of regular Freemasonry that operates under its own Grand Lodges. Our lodge, AUL #14, is under the jurisdiction of the Most Worshipful Prince Hall Grand Lodge of the State of New York.

What are the requirements to become a Mason?

To petition for membership, a man must: believe in a Supreme Being, be at least 18 years of age, be of good moral character, and come of his own free will. Freemasonry does not solicit members — you must ask. We welcome men of every race, creed, and background.

Is Freemasonry a religion?

No. Freemasonry is not a religion, nor is it a substitute for religion. While a belief in a Supreme Being is required, the fraternity does not promote any particular faith or denomination. Brothers of all faiths are welcome and encouraged to practice their own religion.

When and where does the lodge meet?

Adelphic Union Lodge #14 holds its Stated Communication on the 4th Tuesday of every month at 454 West 155th Street, New York, NY 10032. The gavel sounds promptly at 7:00 PM. Visitors who are Master Masons in good standing are always welcome.

How can I support the lodge without being a member?

You can support our work through donations (CashApp: $AUL14, Zelle: AdelphicUnionLodge14@gmail.com), attending public events, volunteering at community service days, or spreading the word about our scholarship program. Friends and family of the lodge are always appreciated.

What are appendant bodies?

Appendant bodies are Masonic organizations that a Master Mason may join for further light and service. These include the Scottish Rite, York Rite (Royal Arch, Cryptic Masons, Knights Templar), the Shriners, and the Order of the Eastern Star (which also admits women). Each offers unique teachings and community service opportunities.

“We represent a fraternity which believes in justice and truth and equal rights to all human beings. Masonry is universal and recognizes that every man has an obligation to uplift his brother.”

Prince Hall, 1797
Masonic Family

Appendant & Concordant Bodies

Our Masonic family extends beyond the Blue Lodge through affiliated organizations.

Scottish Rite
Ancient and Accepted Scottish Rite, PHA. The philosophical arm of Masonry.
Shriners
AEAONMS. Known for their philanthropic children's hospitals.
Order of the Eastern Star
Open to men and women. Committed to charitable endeavors and service.
York Rite
Royal Arch, Cryptic Masons, and Knights Templar. A pathway of deeper knowledge.
